questions.prompt: Printix's three-letter printer IDs (ASD, BNM, CVB) are randomly assigned per registration so users can identify a specific printer regardless of its location name.
questions.explanation: Printix prepends a unique three-letter ID to every registered printer. The same ID appears on the printer's QR code or NFC tag, so users can find the right queue by scanning.

questions.prompt: When the Printix Client adds a print queue for a user, where does the print driver come from?
choices: The technician copies it from a shared drive before delivery
The Printix Cloud's driver store, downloaded automatically
Windows Update finds and installs it
The printer pushes its driver via SNMP
questions.explanation: Printix maintains a global driver store and the Printix Client downloads the right driver automatically when a queue is installed. That removes the technician-trip-with-USB-drive workflow.
